scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance for Research & Development Activities: Assistance for Contract/Sponsored Research Work
page_title: Scheme for Assistance for Research & Development Activities: Assistance for Contract/Sponsored Research Work

details: The "Scheme for Assistance for Research & Development Activities" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat under the Gujarat Industrial Policy. Effective from August 7th, 2020 to August 6th, 2025, this initiative promotes sustainable industrial growth by supporting industries in adopting eco-friendly practices and ensuring compliance with environmental norms.

The component "Assistance for Contract/Sponsored Research Work" provides financial support to eligible institutions for establishing R&D or product development centers. It aims to promote innovation, technological advancements, and product design improvements.
benefits: Up to 50% of the project cost (excluding land and building costs).
Facilitates technological advancements through sponsored research.
eligibility: All Research and Development Institutions recognized by the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) / Department of Scientific and Industrial Research (DSIR).
Institutions set up by the Government of Gujarat (GoG) / Government of India (Gol).
All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) approved institutions/professional colleges, (including autonomous as well as those colleges affiliated with Universities).
Any other institution as approved by SLEC.
Proposals for contract/sponsored research by eligible institutions from Industrial Associations or individual industrial undertaking or government or any other agency as approved by SLEC.

offline: The R&D institution must apply to the Industries Commissionerate in the
The office of the Industries Commissioner will scrutinize the proposals to ensure compliance with the scheme's eligibility and guidelines.
The proposals will be presented to the State Level Empowered Committee (SLEC) for review and approval.
Upon approval by SLEC, the reimbursement of the sanctioned assistance will be processed, and the claim will be disbursed by the office of the Industries Commissioner.

documents_required: Copy of Project Sanction Letter Issued by IC office.
Copy of Registration of the Institution / Industry / enterprise / Academia / Professional Body issued by competent authority along with MoM / AoA filed with the competent authority.
Copy of CSIR/ DSIR Recognition Letter.
Details of Sponsoring Industries / Institution / Organization.
Undertaking / Agreement of beneficiary industry/ promoter to share proportionate cost of Contract Research Work.

Project Progress Report along with the details of milestones achieved, Outcomes of R&D proposed, Details of Asset creation, Benefit to the society, Benefit to the Industries, Details of Patent Filed / Granted, Details of International / National Publications, Impact analysis of the R&D and other relevant details related to the project.
Employment Details of Technical qualified manpower in Prescribed format.
Project Completion Certificate (PCC) in Prescribed format.
Copy of GR, Notification, If the organization is established by Government of India or Government of Gujarat.
Notarized undertaking for fulfilment of the conditions mentioned in the Sanction letter along with copy of relevant Board Resolution / Competent Authority in Prescribed format.
Bank Details / Cancel cheque for assistance to be received under this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the "Scheme for Assistance for Research & Development Activities" scheme?
faqs.answer: The "Scheme for Assistance for Research & Development Activities" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat under the Gujarat Industrial Policy. Effective from August 7th, 2020 to August 6th, 2025, this initiative promotes sustainable industrial growth by supporting industries in adopting eco-friendly practices and ensuring compliance with environmental norms.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the component "Assistance for Contract/Sponsored Research Work"?
faqs.answer: The component "Assistance for Contract/Sponsored Research Work" provides financial support to eligible institutions for establishing R&D or product development centers. It aims to promote innovation, technological advancements, and product design improvements.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the assistance for contract/sponsored research work?
faqs.answer: Eligible institutions recognized by CSIR/DSIR, those set up by the Government of Gujarat (GoG) or Government of India (GoI), AICTE-approved institutions, and professional colleges are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: Can an individual apply for the assistance?
faqs.answer: No, the assistance is available only to eligible institutions in collaboration with industrial associations, government bodies, or other approved sponsors.

faqs.question: What is the maximum amount of assistance available under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The maximum assistance provided is ₹50,00,000/- per project during the operative period of the scheme.

faqs.question: Are land and building costs eligible for funding under the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, the assistance excludes costs related to land and buildings.

faqs.question: What percentage of the total project cost is covered by the assistance?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers 50% of the total project cost, excluding land and building expenses.

faqs.question: Is there a specific format required for submitting the research proposal?
faqs.answer: Yes, institutions must submit a detailed research proposal according to the format specified by the SLEC.

faqs.question: Can an institution apply for more than one project under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, institutions can apply for multiple projects, provided each project meets the eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: Is there a specific cost breakdown format required for the proposal?
faqs.answer: Yes, the cost breakdown must exclude land and building expenses and adhere to the financial guidelines set by the SLEC.

faqs.question: Can the assistance be used to purchase equipment for the research?
faqs.answer: Yes, the assistance can be used for research-related expenses, including equipment, but it cannot be used for land and building costs.
